title: Tokens Per Second
description: The serving throughput metric that measures how many model tokens a system generates per second over a chosen measurement window.

whatItIs.title: What It Is
whatItIs.body: Tokens per second (tokens/s, tok/s, or TPS) is a throughput rate: how many model tokens a serving system generates per second over the measurement window you choose. It counts generated tokens, not human words or characters, and it describes aggregate output pace rather than one reader's wait time.

whyItMatters.title: Why It Matters
whyItMatters.body: Operators use tokens per second to compare how much generation work a deployment can sustain. Higher throughput can mean better hardware utilization or more concurrent answers, but the headline number depends on batching, prompt length, and whether the window includes prefill, decode, or both.

simpleExample.title: Simple Example
simpleExample.body: If a server emits 120 generated tokens in two seconds of steady streaming, that slice averages 60 tokens per second. The same deployment might report a different rate over a longer window that also includes queueing, prompt prefill, or idle gaps between requests.

commonConfusions.title: Common Confusions
commonConfusions.body: Tokens per second is not the same as total request latency, time to first token, or inter-token latency. Latency metrics describe waits and gaps for one stream. Throughput describes how many tokens move through the system per second, often averaged across many tokens, requests, or a benchmark window.
